metadata.title: LLM Wiki
metadata.description: Free, open-source implementation of Karpathy's LLM Wiki. Upload documents and build a compounding wiki directly via Claude.
metadata.loginDescription: Sign in to LLM Wiki to manage your knowledge bases and wikis.
metadata.signupDescription: Create a free LLM Wiki account. Upload documents and build a compounding wiki powered by Claude.
metadata.termsDescription: Terms of Service for LLM Wiki.

landing.heroSubtitle: Your LLM compiles and maintains a structured wiki from raw sources.
landing.previewText: This wiki tracks research on transformer architectures and their scaling properties. It synthesizes findings from <sources>12 sources</sources> across 47 pages.
landing.keyFindingsText: The relationship between model size and performance follows predictable <scalingLaws>scaling laws</scalingLaws> — loss decreases as a power law of compute, dataset size, and parameter count.
landing.update1: Added analysis of sparse attention variants
landing.update2: Updated scaling laws with new benchmarks
landing.update3: Flagged contradiction between Chen et al. and Wei et al.
landing.threeLayersDesc: You rarely ever write the wiki yourself — the wiki is the domain of the LLM.
landing.rawSourcesDesc: Articles, papers, notes, transcripts. Your immutable source of truth. The LLM reads from them but never modifies them.
landing.theWikiDesc: LLM-generated markdown pages with summaries, entity pages, and cross-references. The LLM owns this layer. You read it; the LLM writes it.
landing.theSchemaDesc: A config file that tells the LLM how the wiki is structured, what conventions to follow, and what workflows to run on ingest.
landing.ingestDesc: Drop a source into raw/. The LLM reads it, writes a summary, updates entity and concept pages across the wiki, and flags anything that contradicts existing knowledge. A single source might touch 10–15 wiki pages.
landing.queryDesc: Ask complex questions against the compiled wiki. Knowledge is already synthesized — not re-derived from raw chunks each time. Good answers get filed back as new pages, so your explorations compound.
landing.lintDesc: Run health checks over the wiki. Find inconsistent data, stale claims, orphan pages, missing cross-references. The LLM suggests new questions to ask and new sources to look for.
landing.quote: The tedious part of maintaining a knowledge base is not the reading or the thinking — it's the bookkeeping. LLMs don't get bored, don't forget to update a cross-reference, and can touch 15 files in one pass.
landing.ctaSubtitle: An incredible product instead of a hacky collection of scripts.

dashboard.createFirstWikiDesc: Upload sources, connect Claude, and let it compile a structured wiki automatically.
dashboard.step1Desc: Name your knowledge space. You can have as many as you need.
dashboard.step2Desc: Upload PDFs, notes, transcripts — anything you want Claude to learn from.
dashboard.step3Desc: Claude reads your sources and compiles a wiki with cross-references and summaries.
dashboard.orCreateCustomName: or create with a custom name

wikis.createWikiDesc: A wiki is an LLM-maintained knowledge base compiled from your raw sources.
wikis.name: Name

settings.localMcpDesc: Run this command to get the Claude Desktop / Claude Code MCP config for this workspace:
settings.oauthMcpDesc: Add this configuration to your MCP client. On first connection, it should prompt you to sign in with Supabase.

onboarding.welcomeDesc: Your LLM compiles and maintains a structured wiki from your raw sources.
onboarding.nameYourWikiDesc: This is your knowledge space. You can rename it anytime.
onboarding.connectClaudeDesc: Add LLM Wiki as a connector so Claude can read and write to your wiki.
onboarding.step4: Paste the URL above and approve access
onboarding.step5: Sign in with your account when prompted
onboarding.skipSetup: Skip — I'll set this up later
onboarding.allSetDesc: Upload some sources to your wiki, then ask Claude to compile them into structured pages.

kb.jumpTo: Jump to page, source, or action...
kb.storageUsageDesc: You've used {used} of your {total} storage limit.
kb.storageNote: Storage is consumed by uploaded files (PDFs, images, office documents). Notes and wiki pages are free and unlimited.
kb.getStartedUpload: Upload documents or create notes to get started
kb.deleteWikiConfirm: This will permanently delete {name} and all its documents. This cannot be undone.
kb.dropFilesHelp: PDF, Word, PowerPoint, images, and more
kb.noWikiYetDesc: Add some sources, then ask Claude to compile a wiki from them.
kb.wikiNotFoundDesc: The wiki “{slug}” does not exist or you don't have access.
